commands.review_summary:
The PR splits grouped taxonomy feature coverage IDs into one-ID feature rows, updates one QA Lab scorecard expectation, and documents the atomic coverage-ID guidance in the claw-score skill, QA docs, and scenario index comments.

PR surface: Tests 0, Docs +13, Other +456. Total +469 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a taxonomy and scorecard cleanup rather than a user-facing bug. The behavior claim is source-checkable against the current `.every` scorecard contract and the PR-head taxonomy split.

target.title: fix: split taxonomy coverage id features
target.body:
## Summary

- Split the remaining grouped taxonomy feature `coverageIds` into single-ID feature rows so coverage IDs are proof targets, not aliases.
- Kept category/profile surfaces and coverage IDs stable while making partial feature proof visible in scorecard output. Generated split rows omit copied parent descriptions to avoid repeating the same prose across every coverage ID.
- Updated the QA Lab profile test expectation for the now-atomic `dm-chat-baseline` feature fulfillment.
- Updated the repo-local claw-score skill to document conjunctive coverage-ID semantics and add fallback audit guidance for taxonomy-only edits while keeping the maturity-docs workflow intact.

## Taxonomy/Scenario Audit

- Taxonomy features: 2013
- Taxonomy coverage IDs: 1660
- Multi-ID taxonomy features: 0
- Empty-coverage taxonomy features: 0
- QA scenario coverage IDs: 160
- QA scenario IDs missing from taxonomy: 0
- Duplicate taxonomy category IDs: 0
- QA scenarios missing primary coverage: 0

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/coverage-report.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/evidence-summary.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs qa coverage --json`
- taxonomy/scenario coverage-ID audit script from the PR summary counts above
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check .agents/skills/claw-score/SKILL.md extensions/qa-lab/src/cli.runtime.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt "Scope: PR 94304 taxonomy coverage-ID cleanup..."` clean, no accepted/actionable findings

Note: `taxonomy.yaml` already fails standalone `oxfmt --check` on `origin/main`; this PR intentionally avoids a whole-file YAML formatter rewrite and validates the taxonomy semantics directly.
